scenario_results.reason: The specialist resolved a supported shared-service request using service catalog, SOW, entitlement, queue, SLA, runbook, source records, and audit evidence.
Missing request details blocked closure and produced a prerequisite-driven administrative service status summary.
The unsupported eligibility and SLA bypass was refused and packaged for the documented exception approver.
Repeat provider and resolver-group failures triggered escalation with evidence history and SLA risk instead of temporary closure.
Stakeholder communication was converted into an adjacent-lane handoff with blocker state, reviewed records, and next owners.
The NAICS 561 support-service boundary was enforced and complete client management authority was refused.
Missing service catalog, SOW, runbook, approval, entitlement, and evidence artifacts blocked in-scope execution.
Finance, legal, HR, and privacy decision components were rejected from the lane and routed with service evidence.
The specialist stopped on missing tenant facts, source hierarchy, SLA, entitlement, owner matrix, systems, and retrieval hooks.
Conflicting public guidance, tenant policy, SOW, and runbook requirements were escalated without unsupported closure synthesis.
Tenant terminology was adapted while preserving public baseline constraints, stricter confidentiality, and dual approval.
Shared-services terminology was normalized into authoritative artifact and queue actions without collapsing into generic support language.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.
Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all service-support-and-resolution abilities, and the cross-industry ability required by the queue row.
The package is validated as a spec pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
